items.headline: U.S. Stocks Retreat Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. equity markets edged lower on Thursday, March 26, 2026, with major indices closing in the red as persistent geopolitical concerns in the Middle East and a surge in crude oil prices weighed heavily on investor sentiment. The Federal Reserve's decision to maintain interest rates also contributed to a cautious trading environment. Defensive sectors like Healthcare showed resilience, while Technology lagged. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Super Micro Computer Inc. Leads Gainers on AI Demand
items.summary: The server and AI infrastructure provider extended recent volatility in the AI supply chain space amid ongoing demand for high-performance computing hardware. Shares rose significantly in early trading.

items.headline: Olaplex Holdings Soars on Acquisition News
items.summary: Shares surged after the company agreed to be acquired by Henkel AG & Co. KGaA for $2.06 per share in cash, representing a substantial premium to its previous close.

items.headline: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Gains on Sector Rotation
items.summary: The enterprise technology firm posted strong gains, benefiting from sector rotation and positive momentum in hardware names tied to data center expansion.

items.headline: ADMA Biologics, Inc. Falls on Short-Seller Report
items.summary: This biotech/pharma stock faced intense pressure following a short-seller report, extending a multi-week slide despite some analysts viewing it as potentially oversold.

items.headline: Wave Life Sciences Ltd. Leads Biotech Decline
items.summary: Wave Life Sciences declined significantly with no immediate identified catalyst, suggesting broader sector-wide pressure or technical resets in the biotech space.

items.headline: MillerKnoll, Inc. Sinks on Weak Earnings and Guidance
items.summary: The company faced significant selling pressure after reporting weaker-than-expected earnings and issuing soft guidance.

items.headline: Sector Performance: Healthcare and Industrials Show Strength, Tech and Energy Lag
items.summary: On March 26, 2026, Healthcare (+0.99%) and Industrials (+0.98%) sectors demonstrated resilience, outperforming broader market indices. Financial Services (+0.72%), Basic Materials (+0.70%), Consumer Cyclical (+0.54%), Real Estate (+0.51%), and Consumer Defensive (+0.46%) also saw positive movement. Conversely, Technology (+0.07%) largely lagged, and Energy (+0.11%) experienced minimal gains despite rising crude prices, influenced by broader market pressures and the re-pricing of Middle East risks.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady Amid Sticky Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained the federal funds rate at 3½ to 3¾ percent on Thursday, March 26, 2026. The decision reflects solid economic growth, a 4.4% unemployment rate, and persistent inflation, with year-end inflation forecast at 2.7%. The FOMC anticipates only one rate cut in 2026.

items.headline: Trade Inflation Surges, Oil Inventories Increase
items.summary: New trade inflation data revealed a significant surge, with import prices climbing 1.3% and export prices rising 1.5% in February, marking the sharpest monthly increase in nearly four years. This unexpected inflationary pressure, partly driven by geopolitical tensions, impacts market sentiment. Separately, U.S. commercial crude oil inventories increased by 6.9 million barrels for the week ending March 20.

items.headline: Mixed Earnings: Braze Beats Revenue, Commercial Metals Reports Weaker Profit
items.summary: Braze ($BRZE) saw its shares soar after reporting better-than-expected top-line results, with revenue jumping 27.9% to $205.2 million. However, the company still needs further improvements for long-term shareholder value. In contrast, Commercial Metals ($CMC) reported a weaker profit for its latest quarter than analysts anticipated, citing adverse weather conditions in North America.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions and Rising Oil Prices Dominate Market Concerns
items.summary: Uncertainty surrounding the ongoing war in Iran continued to weigh heavily on Wall Street. Crude oil prices surged, with Brent crude climbing 4.5% to $101.62 per barrel and U.S. West Texas Intermediate futures rising 4.3% to $94.19 per barrel, as hopes dimmed for a swift resolution to regional conflicts and potential disruptions to the Strait of Hormuz.
